title: Hut 8 Pivots From Bitcoin to AI With $7B Google-Backed Deal to Power Data Centers
text: Hut 8 announced a major pivot from Bitcoin mining to AI data center operations with a $7 billion Google-backed deal. This agreement marks a watershed moment in the company's transformation into a primary AI infrastructure provider. Google Connection: The deal is backed by Google, which acts as a financial backstop for the arrangement. Google's involvement underscores the legitimacy and scale of Hut 8's AI infrastructure ambitions. Deal Structure: The agreement involves leasing capacity at Hut 8's River Bend campus in Louisiana to Fluidstack, an AI cloud infrastructure company. The arrangement covers 245 megawatts of computing capacity designed for AI workloads. Strategic Implications: This deal positions Hut 8 as a critical infrastructure provider for AI companies, moving beyond commodity mining toward specialized, high-margin data center services. The multi-billion dollar contract provides revenue visibility for years. Broader Context: Hut 8's pivot reflects industry-wide recognition that AI infrastructure will drive higher-value business than Bitcoin mining, particularly as network difficulty and block rewards continue to compress margins.
